tweets.text: Auction houses could fix this by giving you timegrapher numbers. But the only auction house that does this so far is a small Swiss one that specializes in watches (Ineichen). None of the big houses do.

tweets.name: Paul Graham
tweets.text: The best way to buy art is at auction, once you know what you're doing. But old watches you should buy from dealers. You can get enough information about paintings to judge their condition, but you have no idea what's inside watches.

tweets.name: Garry Tan

A bunch of people are starting to dunk on this as if us releasing this were bad. If you're 18 and a brilliant engineer, you aren't born with this. This is 101, basic knowledge.

And is it complicated? Yes Accounting you could spend a lifetime learning! Hard to distill to 1 page.

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text: I use Slack too much with AI not to think about this.

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text: If Slack were built from the ground up today, what would it be like?

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text: If you don’t use the best available technology to serve customers better, someone else will. That is the oldest law in business. The only thing that changes is how long customers have to wait for a better option. Right now, that wait is getting shorter.

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text:
This gets at something I see all the time.

Experience only stays valuable when it keeps updating.

AI lowers the cost of trying, learning, and changing your mind. That makes stale judgment easier to spot.

The most effective operators treat experience like a living system. It https://t.co/47YJPL2Nxn
